en.extract:
The north Melanesian cuckooshrike (Coracina welchmani) is a species of bird in the cuckooshrike family. It is endemic to the Solomon Islands archipelago. It is considered by some ornithologists to be a subspecies of Coracina caledonica.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy

The syntypes of Graucalus (Artamides) welchmani Tristram (Ibis, 1891, p.294), an adult male and a pullus, is held in the vertebrate zoology collection of National Museums Liverpool at World Museum, with accession numbers NML-VZ T16743 and NML-VZ T16749. The specimen was collected in Bugotu Island, Solomon Islands in December 1870 by Dr. Welchman. The specimen came to the Liverpool national collection came to the Liverpool national collection through the purchase of Canon Henry Baker Tristram's collection by the museum in 1896.

The '''north Melanesian cuckooshrike''' ('''''Coracina welchmani''''')<ref>[http://www.worldbirdnames.org/n-batises.html  Worldbirdnames.org] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090213113340/http://www.worldbirdnames.org/n-batises.html |date=2009-02-13 }}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the [[cuckooshrike]] family. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Solomon Islands (archipelago)|Solomon Islands archipelago]]. It is considered by some [[ornithologist]]s to be a [[subspecies]] of ''[[Coracina caledonica]]''.<ref>[http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=0A14231E84EBE00A Avibase.bsc-eoc.org]</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.fatbirder.com/species_and_families/passerines/campephagidae.html |title=Fatbirder.com |access-date=2010-10-09 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140903132227/http://fatbirder.com/species_and_families/passerines/campephagidae.html |archive-date=2014-09-03 }}</ref>
Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] moist [[montane forest]]s.

== Taxonomy ==
[[File:NML-VZ T16749 Graucalus (Artamides) welchmani.jpg|thumb|Syntype of ''Graucalus (Artamides) welchman''i Tristram (NML-VZ T16749) held at World Museum, National Museums Liverpool]]
The [[syntype]]s of ''Graucalus (Artamides) welchmani'' Tristram ([https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/16188367 Ibis, 1891, p.294]), an adult male and a pullus, is held in the vertebrate zoology collection of [[National Museums Liverpool]] at [[World Museum]], with accession numbers NML-VZ T16743 and NML-VZ T16749. The specimen was collected in Bugotu Island, [[Solomon Islands]] in December 1870 by Dr. Welchman. The specimen came to the Liverpool national collection came to the Liverpool national collection through the purchase of Canon [[Henry Baker Tristram]]'s collection by the museum in 1896.<ref>{{Cite book|last=R. Wagstaffe|url=http://archive.org/details/type-specimens-of-birds-in-the-merseyside-county-museums-wagstaffe|title=Type Specimens of Birds in the Merseyside County Museums (formerly City of Liverpool Museums)|date=1978-12-01}}</ref>
